name: Titan Payday Loans
description_short: Titan Payday Loans is a multi-state loan referral network connecting bad-credit borrowers with lenders for auto, motorcycle, and boat financing up to $10,000.
description_long:
Titan Payday Loans operates as a loan request submission network with a footprint spanning more than 40 cities across roughly 20 states. Despite the 'payday loans' branding, the company's primary offerings are secured installment loans tied to vehicles—cars, motorcycles, and boats—marketed toward borrowers who have been declined elsewhere. The Seattle location listed at 654 Union St #4200 is one of dozens of addresses the company maintains nationwide, all following a similar virtual-office format.

The platform offers three core loan types: car bad credit loans, motorcycle bad credit loans, and boat bad credit loans. Borrowers submit a free inquiry form online and are matched with lenders in the network. Titan claims loan amounts up to $5,000 on the page header and up to $10,000 in the footer call-to-action—an inconsistency that is not explained on the site. The company positions itself as a connector between borrowers and lenders rather than a direct lender, which means actual loan terms, APR, and repayment schedules are set by third-party lenders, not Titan itself.

Titan's stated differentiators include an open-door policy toward borrowers with poor or thin credit histories, a no-hidden-fees-from-the-borrower pledge (fees from the lending side are not addressed), and 256-bit SSL with TLS encryption for application data security. The site also encourages rate shopping, explicitly stating that comparisons are welcome before signing any contract.

Honestly, Titan Payday Loans shows the hallmarks of a lead-generation aggregator: a templated site replicated across dozens of city-specific URLs, virtual office suite addresses with no physical walk-in presence, zero disclosed APR ranges or fee schedules, and inconsistent loan amount claims within the same page. Borrowers with bad credit seeking vehicle-secured financing may receive leads through this network, but they should independently verify any lender they are matched with, read all disclosures carefully, and compare offers before committing.
address: Armstrong Cork Factory - Factory Building, 2349 Railroad St, Pittsburgh, PA 15222

company_info.headquarters: Armstrong Cork Factory - Factory Building, 2349 Railroad St, Pittsburgh, PA 15222

pros: Accepts many credit types, including borrowers with poor or no credit history
Free to submit a loan inquiry form—no upfront cost to apply
Claims no hidden fees charged directly to the borrower
Encourages borrowers to compare offers before signing
Application data protected with 256-bit SSL and TLS encryption
Broad geographic coverage across 20+ states with localized contact numbers
Covers multiple collateral types: cars, motorcycles, and boats
cons: No APR, interest rate range, or fee schedule disclosed anywhere on the site
Operates as a lead-generation referral network, not a direct lender—actual loan terms depend entirely on third-party lenders
Inconsistent loan amount claims: $5,000 in the header versus $10,000 in the footer CTA
All listed addresses appear to be virtual office suites with no walk-in branch presence
Generic, template-style content replicated across dozens of city pages offers no city-specific information
best_for: Borrowers with bad credit seeking vehicle-secured financing (auto, motorcycle, boat)
Consumers who have been declined by traditional lenders and need a broad network referral
Borrowers comfortable applying entirely online with no in-person support
diagnosis: Titan Payday Loans is best suited for bad-credit borrowers who need a secured vehicle loan and want to cast a wide net across multiple lenders via a single online form. The main caveat is that Titan is a referral intermediary, not a lender—no rates or fees are disclosed upfront, so borrowers must independently evaluate every lender they are matched with before agreeing to any terms.
